title: Houses for Rent in Strongsville, OH
metaTitle: Houses for Rent in Strongsville, Ohio
metaDescription: Looking for houses for rent in Strongsville, Ohio? See typical Cleveland-area rent ranges, housing stock, and how to tell us what you need.
answerBox: Strongsville, Ohio is a southwest Cuyahoga County suburb (ZIP 44136) along I-71 near State Route 82, with mostly newer suburban single-family housing. Rents across Greater Cleveland typically run $700-$1,800 a month. Strongsville isn't currently in our selection, but tell us your bedroom count and budget and we'll compare nearby options.

sections.h2: Renting a house in Strongsville, Ohio
body: Strongsville is a southwest Cuyahoga County suburb bordered by North Royalton to the east and Brunswick, in Medina County, to the south, with **ZIP code 44136** covering most of the city. Interstate 71 runs through Strongsville with a major interchange at State Route 82, and the housing stock here leans toward newer suburban construction — single-family ranch and colonial homes built mostly from the 1960s through the 1990s on larger lots than the century-old housing common closer to Cleveland proper.

**Rent Finder Cleveland works with 90+ voucher-friendly rental homes concentrated in Cleveland's east and southeast side neighborhoods, and Strongsville is not currently part of our selection.** If you're set on Strongsville specifically, a Strongsville-focused property manager or listing site will have more direct inventory. If your search is flexible on location, [tell us what you're looking for](/p/book-showing) — bedrooms, budget, and whether you hold a housing voucher — and we can point you to comparable options in the Cleveland neighborhoods we do work with.

sections.h2: What does it cost to rent in the Cleveland area?
body: Strongsville rents tend to run higher than the Cleveland city average given its newer housing stock and larger lots, but as a general Greater Cleveland benchmark, Zumper's July 4, 2026 report puts the metro's median asking rent at **$1,250/month** overall, with 2-bedroom units around **$1,100** and 3-bedroom units around **$1,350**. RentCafe's July 2, 2026 report, which weights toward larger professionally-managed buildings, shows a higher metro average of **$1,564/month**. Actual Strongsville house rents will depend on the specific home, its size, and condition.

Strongsville sits along Interstate 71 at the State Route 82 interchange, giving direct highway access north toward downtown Cleveland and south toward Medina County. SouthPark Mall and the surrounding retail corridor along State Route 42 (Pearl Road) form the city's main commercial district, and Mill Stream Run Reservation, part of the Cleveland Metroparks system, runs along the eastern edge of the city. Greater Cleveland RTA's fixed bus routes are concentrated closer to the city and inner-ring suburbs, so most Strongsville residents rely on a car for daily transportation.

items: **Highways:** I-71 at the State Route 82 interchange
**Location:** Southwest Cuyahoga County, bordering North Royalton and Brunswick

sections.h2: Section 8 and voucher holders looking in Strongsville
body: Ohio has no statewide law requiring landlords to accept Housing Choice Vouchers, and Strongsville is not among the handful of Cuyahoga County communities — Cleveland Heights, South Euclid, University Heights, Warrensville Heights, and Linndale — that have adopted local source-of-income protection ordinances, so acceptance in Strongsville depends on the individual landlord. The [Cuyahoga Metropolitan Housing Authority (CMHA)](https://www.cmha.net/housing/applicants/housing_choice_vouchers.php) administers the countywide Housing Choice Voucher program and accepts preliminary applications year-round. Every home Rent Finder Cleveland works with accepts Section 8 and is HUD-inspection-ready, though our current homes are concentrated in Cleveland rather than Strongsville — see our [Section 8 guide](/section-8-housing-cleveland-oh/) for how the program works locally.

If your search spans both Strongsville and Cleveland proper, our [houses for rent in Cleveland](/houses-for-rent-cleveland-oh/) pillar page covers the neighborhoods where we work with inventory today. You can also compare nearby [North Olmsted](/cleveland-rentals/suburbs/houses-for-rent-north-olmsted-ohio/), or [tell us what you need](/p/book-showing) and we'll help you weigh it against homes we do work with.

faq.q: Does Rent Finder Cleveland works with houses in Strongsville, Ohio?
faq.a: Not currently. Our 90+ voucher-friendly rental homes are concentrated in Cleveland's east and southeast side neighborhoods, and Strongsville is outside our current selection. If you're flexible on location, tell us your bedroom count and budget and we'll point you to comparable options we do work with.

faq.q: How much does it cost to rent a house in Strongsville, Ohio?
faq.a: Strongsville-specific pricing varies by home and size, but as a Greater Cleveland benchmark, Zumper's July 2026 report shows a metro median of about $1,100 for 2-bedroom and $1,350 for 3-bedroom units, while RentCafe's July 2026 report runs higher for larger professionally-managed buildings.

faq.q: What ZIP code is Strongsville, Ohio in?
faq.a: Strongsville is primarily ZIP code 44136, in southwest Cuyahoga County, bordering North Royalton and Brunswick along Interstate 71.

faq.q: Do Strongsville landlords accept Section 8 vouchers?
faq.a: It depends on the individual landlord. Ohio has no statewide law requiring voucher acceptance, and Strongsville is not among the small number of Cuyahoga County communities with a local source-of-income protection ordinance. CMHA administers the countywide Housing Choice Voucher program.

faq.q: How do I get from Strongsville to downtown Cleveland?
faq.a: Interstate 71 runs directly through Strongsville and connects north into downtown Cleveland. Greater Cleveland RTA's fixed-route bus network is concentrated closer to the city and inner-ring suburbs, so most Strongsville residents drive for the commute.
